zh.Powderham Castle, Devon, England: 英國戴文波德哈姆 卡素
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John de Courtenay, Knight of Powderham於大約1384年出生在英國戴文波德哈姆時，他的父親Sir Philip Courtenay41歲，母親Anne Wake32歲。他於1403年與Joan Champernowne在英國戴文波德哈姆 卡素結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1415年之前在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國戴文波德哈姆。

zh-hans.Powderham Castle, Devon, England: 英国戴文波德哈姆 卡素
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John de Courtenay, Knight of Powderham于大约1384年出生在英国戴文波德哈姆时，他的父亲Sir Philip Courtenay41岁，母亲Anne Wake32岁。他于1403年与Joan Champernowne在英国戴文波德哈姆 卡素结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1415年之前在他的家乡，埋葬在英国戴文波德哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Courtenay,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Courtney  .

variants.name: Courtney
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Courtenay near Sens in northern France, or some other place similarly named, from the name of a Gallo-Roman landlord, Curtenus (a derivative of Latin curtus ‘short’) + the locative suffix -acum.
Irish: English surname adopted for Ó Curnáin ‘descendant of Curnán’, an Old Irish personal name based on a diminutive of corn ‘horn’.

History: A family of this name (see 1 above) can be traced back to Athon de Courtenay, who lived in Courtenay in Loiret in the early 11th century. Renaud de Courtenay held land in Devon in the 12th century, and the family later held the title of Earls of Devon following the marriage of Hugh de Courtenay to Margaret de Bohun, granddaughter of King Edward I, in 1325.

nameMeanings.name: Knight
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John de Courtenay, Knight of Powderham was born about 1384, in Powderham, Devon, England, his father, Sir Philip Courtenay, was 41 and his mother, Anne Wake, was 32. He married Joan Champernowne in 1403, in Powderham Castle, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died before 1415, in his hometown, and was buried in Powderham, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John de Courtenay, Knight of Powderham est né environ 1384, à Powderham, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Sir Philip Courtenay, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Anne Wake, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Joan Champernowne en 1403, à Powderham Castle, Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé avant 1415, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Powderham, Devon, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John de Courtenay, Knight of Powderham um 1384 in Powderham, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Philip Courtenay 41 und seine Mutter Anne Wake 32. Er hat 1403 in Powderham Castle, Devon, England Joan Champernowne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb vor 1415 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Powderham, Devon, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Courtenay, Knight of Powderham è nato il circa 1384 nel Powderham, Devon, England, il suo padre, Sir Philip Courtenay, aveva 41 anni e il suo madre, Anne Wake, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Joan Champernowne nel 1403, nel Powderham Castle, Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì prima del 1415, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Powderham, Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John de Courtenay, Knight of Powderhamが約1384年にイギリスデボンポウデルハムで生まれた時，父Sir Philip Courtenayは41歳，母Anne Wakeは32歳であった。1403年にイギリスデボンポウデルハム カストルでJoan Champernowneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1415年以前に故郷で死去し，イギリスデボンポウデルハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Courtenay, Knight of Powderham(이)가 대략 1384년 영국 데폰 파우더함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Philip Courtenay(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Wake(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1403년 영국 데폰 파우더함 카스틀에서 Joan Champernowne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1415년 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 데폰 파우더함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Courtenay, Knight of Powderham nasceu aproximadamente 1384, em Powderham, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Philip Courtenay, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Anne Wake, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Champernowne em 1403, em Powderham Castle, Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu antes 1415, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Powderham, Devon, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John de Courtenay, Knight of Powderham родился около 1384 г. в местности поудирам, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Philip Courtenay было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Anne Wake, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Champernowne в 1403 г. в местности поудирам кастл, девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер перед 1415 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности поудирам, девон, Англия.

sm.about 1384–Bef 1415: e tusa o le 1384–a'e le'I oo le 1415

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John de Courtenay, Knight of Powderham nació aproximadamente 1384, en Powderham, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Philip Courtenay, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Anne Wake, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Joan Champernowne en 1403, en Powderham Castle, Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió antes de 1415, en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Powderham, Devon, Inglaterra.
